name: Reasons for Adding Gluten or Ascorbic Acid to Flour.
recipeInstructions:
1. Most people add gluten if they're using low-gluten flour, especially wheat flours.
2. Where I live, bread flour is really hard to find and extremely expensive (at least four or five times more than all-purpose), so it's cheaper for me to use all-purpose most of the time and add gluten, rather than buying bread flour.
3. Adding a little ascorbic acid gives your bread an extra boost, even if there's already some in the flour.
4. It makes the bread less dense.
5. The acidity is also supposed to make the bread last longer.
